revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天照皇御祖神社]]
== Amaterasu Sumemioya Shrine == {{Infobox religious building |name=Amaterasu Sumemioya Shrine |image=File:Amaterasu-sumemioya-jinja 20211009a.jpg |location={{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|qid=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kazuno, Akita|en|Kazuno, Akita|qid=Q496499|lt=Kazuno|1=Kazuno, Akita}} Hachimantai Aza Yachi 14 |coordinates={{Coord|40.11055555555556|N|140.79033333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, Dainichireki-no-kami, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|qid=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}}, {{ill|Ōtomo-wake-no-mikoto|ja|大鞆和気命|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo-wake-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Emperor Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q317997}} |annual festival=August 16 |status=Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |prefecture={{ill|Kazuno|qid=Q496499|1=Kazuno, Akita}} }} '''Amaterasu Sumemioya Shrine''' (天照皇御祖神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kazuno, Akita|en|Kazuno, Akita|qid=Q496499|lt=Kazuno|1=Kazuno, Akita}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|qid=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}}, Japan. It hosts traditional performances such as the "Sakibarai-mai" (designated as an intangible folk cultural property by the city) and the "Godaison-mai," part of the UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage and Japan's Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property known as {{ill|Dainichido_Bugaku|en|Dainichido_Bugaku|qid=Q11435617|lt=Dainichido Bugaku|1=Dainichido Bugaku}}. The {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|1=Magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11239248}} in the shrine grounds is considered the northernmost of its kind from the medieval period and is a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Traditionally known as Yachi Kannon-dō, the shrine has a history marked by two fires, leaving the exact founding date unclear. However, the stone pagoda (ita-hi) in the precincts dates back over 700 years, suggesting the shrine predates this marker. * 1564 (Eiroku 7) - Yachi Kannon-dō burned during a conflict involving Akita Chikahide. * 1574 (Tenshō 2) - Rebuilt as Amaterasu Sumemioya Shrine. * 1659 (Manji 2) - Rebuilt. * 1785 (Tenmei 5) - Rebuilt again. * 1873 (Meiji 6) - Designated as a village shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} (Amaterasu-ōmikami) * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-mikoto) * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukemochi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 保食神|1=Ukemochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2280323}} (Ukemochi-no-mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-mikoto|ja|誉田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Emperor Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q317997}} (Hondawake-no-mikoto) == Major Festivals == === Sakibarai-mai === Held annually on August 15–16. The sacred object is carried from Amaterasu Sumemioya Shrine to Yasaka Shrine in Hachimantai, Kazuno City. Children perform dances leading the portable shrine procession, cleansing the area along the route. This ritual is designated as an intangible folk cultural property by Kazuno City. === Godaison-mai === Formally offered on December 26, this dance is part of {{ill|Dainichido Bugaku|en|Dainichido Bugaku|qid=Q11435617|lt=Dainichidō Bugaku|1=Dainichido Bugaku}}, recognized as a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage and an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. It represents the story of the legendary Danburi Chōja. == Magaibutsu and Stone Pagodas == The Magaibutsu, located on the eastern side of the shrine grounds, features carvings of the Amida Triad and dates back to the late Kamakura period. The central figure is Amida Nyorai, flanked by Kannon Bodhisattva and Seishi Bodhisattva. This site is the northernmost example of such medieval carvings and is designated as a prefectural historic site. The stone pagodas (ita-hi) bear inscriptions dating to the late Kamakura period, with one marked from 1313 (Shōwa 2). The style resembles those found in Musashi Province, likely linked to the presence of samurai clans such as Narita, Anbo, Nara, and Akimoto, who settled in the Kazuno area. == Yasaka Shrine (Kazuno) == {{Infobox religious building |name=Yasaka Shrine (Kazuno Yasaka Shrine) |image=[[File:Yasaka-jinja (Kazuno).jpg|240px|Kazuno Yasaka Shrine|en]] |location={{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|qid=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kazuno, Akita|en|Kazuno, Akita|qid=Q496499|lt=Kazuno|1=Kazuno, Akita}} Hachimantai Aza Kaminaeshiro 22-1 |coordinates={{coord|40|06|54.0|N|140|47|20.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-05|display=inline|qq=}} |enshrined deity={{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売命|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}} |annual festival=August 16 }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社), also known as '''Kazuno Yasaka Shrine''' (鹿角八坂神社), is a detached precinct shrine of Amaterasu Sumemioya Shrine, located slightly north. It serves as the {{ill|Otabisho|en|Otabisho|qid=Q11489117|lt=Otabisho|1=Otabisho}} during the Sakibarai-mai festival. Although categorized as a Yasaka Shrine, it also enshrines deities from Inari Shrines. Despite its small size, it is popular for blessings related to matchmaking and offers various amulets, {{ill|Shuin|lt=Shuin|lt=Goshuin|ja|御朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshuin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱印 (神社仏閣)}}, and prayer services. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://tennoudou.wixsite.com/amaterasusumemioya/ Amaterasu Sumemioya Shrine Official Website] * [https://tennoudou.wixsite.com/yasakazinja Yasaka Shrine Official Website] * [http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/kaduno/03_amaterasusumemioya.html Amaterasu Sumemioya Shrine - Akita Prefectural Shrine Office] ]]]] {{translated page|ja|天照皇御祖神社|version=99052615|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:天照皇御祖神社]] [[ja:八坂神社 (鹿角市)]]
